KNOWLEDGE_TYPE_UNIVERSAL_INTUITIVE_DESC: Basic survival knowledge that is nearly impossible to lose. Anyone can learn by observation.
KNOWLEDGE_TYPE_COMMON_PRACTICE_DESC: Widely practiced community skills. Requires a minimum number of active practitioners.
KNOWLEDGE_TYPE_CRAFT_TACIT_DESC: Specialized skills passed through master-apprentice chains. Vulnerable if the chain breaks.
KNOWLEDGE_TYPE_ELITE_SCHOLARLY_DESC: Expert knowledge requiring formal education and institutional support.
KNOWLEDGE_TYPE_INSTITUTIONAL_STATE_DESC: Knowledge embedded in organizations and roles. Collapses when institutions fail.
KNOWLEDGE_TYPE_INFRASTRUCTURE_NETWORKED_: Knowledge dependent on physical infrastructure and trade networks.
TECH_LOST_FMT: The knowledge of {tech} has been lost...
TECH_REGRESSION_WARNING_FMT: Knowledge of {tech} is fading — only {count} practitioners remain.
TECH_STABILIZED_FMT: Knowledge of {tech} is now firmly established in {settlement}.
TECH_ANIMISM_DESC: The belief that spirits inhabit natural objects and forces, giving early humans a framework to interpret and negotiate with the world around them.
TECH_BASIC_FISHING_DESC: Simple hand-fishing, spearing, and line techniques that allow communities to harvest the bounty of rivers and shorelines.
TECH_BASIC_HUNTING_DESC: The coordinated pursuit and killing of game animals using simple weapons and group encirclement.
TECH_BASIC_TOOLS_DESC: The earliest shaped stones and sticks — scrapers, choppers, and handaxes — extending human reach and capability.
TECH_BONE_TOOLS_DESC: Fashioning needles, awls, and hooks from animal bone, unlocking finer work than stone alone permits.
TECH_CONTROLLED_BURN_DESC: Deliberately setting fire to land to drive game, clear brush, and encourage fresh growth — humanity's first act of landscape management.
TECH_COOKING_DESC: Applying fire to food to kill pathogens, improve nutrition, and soften tough plant matter — fundamentally reshaping the human diet.
TECH_CORDAGE_DESC: Twisting plant fibers into rope and twine, enabling traps, hafting, shelter binding, and a dozen other crafts.
TECH_FIRE_MAKING_DESC: Reliably producing fire through friction or percussion — light, warmth, and protection on demand.
TECH_FLUTE_MAKING_DESC: Carving hollow bone or reed into musical instruments, evidence of a mind capable of art, ritual, and abstract expression.
TECH_GATHERING_KNOWLEDGE_DESC: Accumulated wisdom about which plants are edible, medicinal, or poisonous, and where and when to find them.
TECH_HIDE_WORKING_DESC: Scraping, stretching, and drying animal hides into serviceable leather for clothing, pouches, and shelter.
TECH_KINSHIP_GROUP_DESC: Organizing society around blood ties and family bonds, establishing trust networks that allow cooperation beyond the individual.
TECH_MICROLITH_TOOLS_DESC: Tiny, precisely knapped flint blades hafted into composite weapons and tools, combining lightness with a deadly cutting edge.
TECH_NUT_PROCESSING_DESC: Techniques for cracking, leaching, and grinding nuts to remove toxins and unlock their dense caloric value.
TECH_OCHRE_PAINTING_DESC: Grinding red and yellow iron oxides into pigment for body decoration, cave art, and ritual marking — the earliest recorded symbolic act.
TECH_ORAL_TRADITION_DESC: Preserving history, law, and lore through memorized songs, stories, and recitations passed faithfully from generation to generation.
TECH_ROOT_DIGGING_DESC: Using sharpened sticks to excavate tubers and roots, accessing calorie-rich plant stores hidden beneath the soil.
TECH_SEED_ROASTING_DESC: Heating wild seeds to improve flavor and digestibility, a practice that would eventually lead to the first deliberate planting.
TECH_SHELTER_BUILDING_DESC: Constructing windbreaks, lean-tos, and simple dwellings from branches and hides, providing reliable refuge from the elements.
TECH_STONE_KNAPPING_DESC: The art of striking flint and obsidian to produce sharp-edged blades and points — the defining skill of the Stone Age toolmaker.
TECH_SWIMMING_DESC: Mastery of water movement enabling river crossing, coastal foraging, and escape from predators or enemies.
TECH_TRACKING_DESC: Reading footprints, broken foliage, and animal sign to follow prey across vast distances — a science encoded in patience.
TECH_TRAPPING_DESC: Setting snares, pit traps, and deadfalls to capture animals passively, multiplying a hunter's effective reach.
TECH_WEATHER_LORE_DESC: Reading cloud patterns, wind shifts, and animal behavior to anticipate rain, storms, and seasonal change.
TECH_ADVANCED_HIDE_WORK_DESC: Brain-tanning, smoking, and softening hides into supple leather garments that resist rot, cold, and abrasion.
TECH_AGRICULTURE_DESC: Cultivating plants for food — the foundation of settled civilization and all that follows from it.
TECH_ANIMAL_DOMESTICATION_DESC: Selectively breeding and taming wild animals over generations until they become dependable partners in labor and sustenance.
TECH_ANIMAL_TRACKING_DESC: Advanced reading of spoor and behavioral signs enabling hunters to anticipate prey movement days in advance.
TECH_ATLATL_DESC: A throwing-board that extends the arm's leverage, launching spears with force enough to pierce thick hide at range.
TECH_BARTER_TRADE_DESC: Exchanging surplus goods between individuals and groups, creating the first rudimentary economic relationships.
TECH_BASKET_WEAVING_DESC: Interlacing plant fibers into containers for gathering, storage, and transport — durable utility from humble materials.
TECH_BEAD_CRAFT_DESC: Drilling and stringing shells, bones, and stones into ornaments that signal status, alliance, and identity.
TECH_BONE_SETTING_DESC: Practical knowledge of splinting and manipulating broken bones so they heal straight and functional.
TECH_BOW_AND_ARROW_DESC: Storing elastic energy in a flexible stave to launch fletched arrows with accuracy and force far beyond any thrown weapon.
TECH_CATTLE_HERDING_DESC: Managing semi-wild aurochs-descended cattle for meat, milk, and draft labor across open grassland.
TECH_CHARCOAL_MAKING_DESC: Slow-burning wood in low-oxygen conditions to produce a fuel that burns hotter and cleaner than raw timber.
TECH_CHIEFTAINSHIP_DESC: Formalizing leadership in a single recognized chief whose authority rests on prowess, generosity, and ancestral prestige.
TECH_COMMUNAL_STORAGE_DESC: Pooling surplus food in a shared granary or cache, allowing communities to survive lean seasons through collective resilience.
TECH_DAIRY_PROCESSING_DESC: Converting animal milk into storable, transportable forms — churning butter, curdling soft cheese — extending its nutritional value.
TECH_DEEP_NETTING_DESC: Weaving and deploying nets in open water to harvest fish in far greater numbers than hook or spear permit.
TECH_DOG_TAMING_DESC: Forging a partnership with wolves over generations, producing the world's first domesticated animal — guard, hunter, and companion.
TECH_DRUM_MAKING_DESC: Stretching hide over a resonant frame to create percussion instruments that anchor ritual, coordinate labor, and carry signals across distance.
TECH_DUGOUT_CANOE_DESC: Hollowing a log by fire and adze into a vessel capable of navigating rivers and calm coastal waters.
TECH_ELDER_COUNCIL_DESC: Institutionalizing the wisdom of age by granting senior community members collective authority over disputes and major decisions.
TECH_FERMENTATION_DESC: Discovered when stored grain or fruit spoiled in just the right way — the accidental invention of beer and wine transformed feasting, ritual, and daily life.
TECH_FISH_TRAP_DESC: Weaving wicker or stone into funnel-shaped enclosures that lure fish in and prevent their escape.
TECH_FLOODPLAIN_SOWING_DESC: Casting seed onto river-deposited silt just as floodwaters recede, exploiting natural fertilization for near-effortless harvests.
TECH_FOOD_PRESERVATION_DESC: Drying, salting, smoking, and fermenting foods to extend their safe storage life through seasons of scarcity.
TECH_GIFT_ECONOMY_DESC: Building social bonds and obligations through ceremonial exchange of gifts, creating networks of reciprocal debt and loyalty.
TECH_HERBALISM_DESC: Systematizing knowledge of medicinal plants — which roots reduce fever, which leaves staunch bleeding, which bark eases pain.
TECH_HORSE_DOMESTICATION_DESC: Taming the steppe horse for riding and draft labor, transforming warfare, trade, and the scale at which humans can move across land.
TECH_KILN_FIRING_DESC: Enclosing a fire to reach temperatures that vitrify clay, producing hard, watertight ceramics far superior to open-fire pottery.
TECH_LEATHER_ARMOR_DESC: Hardening and layering cured leather into body protection that deflects glancing blows without the weight of metal.
TECH_LLAMA_DOMESTICATION_DESC: Domesticating the Andean camelid as a pack animal and fiber source, enabling high-altitude trade routes where no other beast could thrive.
TECH_LUNAR_CALENDAR_DESC: Counting the moon's cycles to track time across months, coordinating planting, festivals, and inter-tribal gatherings.
TECH_MARRIAGE_ALLIANCE_DESC: Using marriage as diplomacy — binding families and clans together in formal kinship, turning potential rivals into obligated kin.
TECH_MUD_BRICK_DESC: Shaping wet clay and straw into sun-dried bricks, enabling construction of permanent, multi-room dwellings in treeless regions.
TECH_NATIVE_COPPER_SMELTING_DESC: Accidentally discovered when hearth stones containing copper ore dripped molten metal — hammered and annealed into tools and ornaments of striking beauty.
TECH_OBSIDIAN_TRADE_DESC: Establishing long-distance exchange networks centered on volcanic glass, whose razor edge made it the most prized cutting material of the ancient world.
TECH_OUTRIGGER_HULL_DESC: Attaching lateral floats to a canoe hull for stability in open ocean, making deep-sea voyaging possible.
TECH_POTTERY_DESC: Shaping and firing clay into vessels for cooking, storage, and water carrying — transforming how communities manage food and resources.
TECH_REED_RAFT_DESC: Bundling buoyant reeds into rafts capable of crossing rivers and navigating shallow coastal waters.
TECH_RENNET_CHEESE_DESC: Stumbled upon when milk stored in a calf's stomach solidified into curds — a happy accident that produced one of humanity's most durable foods.
TECH_RITUAL_DANCE_DESC: Codifying movement, rhythm, and costume into ceremonies that bind communities, honor spirits, and mark life's transitions.
TECH_SELECTIVE_BREEDING_DESC: Deliberately choosing which animals and plants reproduce, slowly bending wild species toward human needs.
TECH_SHAMANISM_DESC: Specialized individuals who enter trance states to communicate with spirits on the community's behalf, mediating between the human and supernatural worlds.
TECH_SHEEP_HERDING_DESC: Managing flocks of domesticated sheep for wool, milk, and meat — a mobile food supply that thrives on marginal land.
TECH_SHELL_CURRENCY_DESC: Using rare, portable shells as a standardized medium of exchange, the first step toward abstract economic value.
TECH_SLASH_AND_BURN_DESC: Clearing forest by cutting and burning to create fertile ash-enriched fields, a method that works well until the forest runs out.
TECH_SLING_DESC: Whirling a leather or woven pouch to hurl stones at lethal velocity — a shepherd's tool that doubled as a weapon of war.
TECH_SPEAR_DESC: A hafted stone or bone point on a long shaft, extending reach and concentrating force into a reliable weapon for hunting and combat.
TECH_STILT_HOUSES_DESC: Raising dwellings on wooden poles above flood level or open water, enabling settlement in wetlands and river deltas.
TECH_STONE_AXE_DESC: A ground and polished stone head hafted to a handle, combining chopping power with a durability that knapped flint cannot match.
TECH_STORYTELLING_EPIC_DESC: Weaving history, myth, and moral teaching into long narrative poems memorized and performed — the archive of pre-literate civilization.
TECH_TABOO_SYSTEM_DESC: Encoding social rules as sacred prohibitions, giving behavioral norms the enforcement power of supernatural sanction.
TECH_TALLY_COUNTING_DESC: Notching bones or sticks to record quantities, the earliest known form of numerical record-keeping.
TECH_TATTOOING_DESC: Marking the body permanently with pigment to indicate status, tribal affiliation, spiritual protection, or rites of passage.
TECH_TERRACE_FARMING_DESC: Cutting level platforms into steep hillsides to create farmable land and prevent erosion where flat ground is scarce.
TECH_TEXTILE_WEAVING_DESC: Interlacing spun fibers on a loom to produce cloth — the basis of clothing trade, wealth, and eventually industrial revolution.
TECH_THATCH_ROOFING_DESC: Layering dried grass or reeds into watertight roofs, providing insulation and shelter using the most abundant local material.
TECH_TREPANATION_DESC: Boring holes in the skull to relieve pressure from head injuries or expel evil spirits — the oldest known surgical procedure.
TECH_TRIBAL_ORGANIZATION_DESC: Structuring society across multiple family groups under shared customs, leadership, and identity — the first step beyond the band.
TECH_WATTLE_AND_DAUB_DESC: Weaving branches into a lattice and plastering it with clay and dung to form sturdy, insulated walls for permanent dwellings.
TECH_WOODEN_CLUB_DESC: The simplest and oldest of weapons — a weighted length of hardwood capable of delivering crushing blows.
TECH_YURT_TENT_DESC: A collapsible felt and lattice-pole dwelling designed to be assembled and struck in hours, perfectly adapted to nomadic steppe life.
TECH_AMBUSH_TACTICS_DESC: Concealing forces to strike at an unprepared enemy from a position of surprise, multiplying effectiveness against numerically superior foes.
TECH_ANATOMY_STUDY_DESC: Systematic observation of the body's internal structures through dissection and wound study, laying the groundwork for rational medicine.
TECH_ANNEALING_DESC: Heating and slowly cooling metal to relieve stress and increase ductility, preventing brittleness in worked bronze.
TECH_BEEKEEPING_DESC: Managing wild bee colonies in log hives to harvest honey and beeswax — the only concentrated sweetener available to ancient peoples.
TECH_BITUMEN_EXTRACTION_DESC: Collecting natural tar seeps for use as waterproof sealant, adhesive, and mortar in boats, baskets, and masonry.
TECH_BOARD_GAMES_DESC: Abstract strategy games played on patterned boards with counters or pieces, training military and political thinking through play.
TECH_BRONZE_ALLOY_DESC: Combining copper and tin in precise ratios to produce an alloy harder and more castable than either metal alone — the defining material of an age.
TECH_BRONZE_SCALE_ARMOR_DESC: Riveting overlapping bronze scales onto a leather backing to create flexible yet protective armor for elite warriors.
TECH_BUREAUCRACY_DESC: Organizing the state through specialized administrators, written records, and standardized procedures — the machinery of large-scale governance.
TECH_CANAL_ENGINEERING_DESC: Digging navigable waterways to connect rivers and cities, enabling the bulk transport of goods that roads cannot match.
TECH_CANAL_IRRIGATION_DESC: Diverting river water through networks of earthen channels to fields, unlocking agricultural production far beyond rainfall alone.
TECH_CARAVAN_DESC: Organizing large groups of pack animals and traders to cross deserts and steppes, making long-distance overland trade viable and defensible.
TECH_CASTE_SYSTEM_DESC: Dividing society into hereditary occupational classes with defined rights and duties, providing social stability at the cost of individual mobility.
TECH_CAVALRY_CHARGE_DESC: Massing mounted warriors for shock impact against infantry — a tactic that dominated battlefields for two thousand years.
TECH_CELESTIAL_NAVIGATION_DESC: Using the positions of stars, sun, and moon to determine direction and location at sea, enabling reliable open-ocean voyaging.
TECH_CHARIOT_DESC: A fast, light two-wheeled vehicle pulled by horses, revolutionizing warfare as a mobile weapons platform and symbol of royal power.
TECH_CODIFIED_LAW_DESC: Writing down legal codes so that rules apply equally and predictably to all citizens, replacing arbitrary judgment with institutional authority.
TECH_COMPOSITE_BOW_DESC: Laminating horn, sinew, and wood into a short, recurved bow that stores more energy and shoots further than any simple wooden equivalent.
TECH_COPPER_SMELTING_DESC: Extracting copper from ore in a furnace, producing metal in quantities sufficient for tools, weapons, and currency.
TECH_CORBEL_ARCH_DESC: Stacking stones in overlapping courses to span an opening — a structural technique that enables roofed passages and chambers without the true arch.
TECH_CROP_ROTATION_DESC: Alternating which crops grow in each field each season to restore soil fertility and break pest cycles.
TECH_CUNEIFORM_DESC: Pressing a reed stylus into clay tablets to record trade, law, and literature — the world's earliest writing system.
TECH_DECIMAL_COUNTING_DESC: A base-ten numbering system that simplifies arithmetic and record-keeping, facilitating trade and administration at scale.
TECH_DEFENSIVE_DITCH_DESC: Encircling a settlement with a deep trench to slow attackers and deny them direct access to walls and gates.
TECH_DEFORESTATION_TRAP_DESC: Clearing forests at scale for farmland and timber. The land yields abundantly — right up until the rains stop holding.
TECH_DIVINE_KINGSHIP_DESC: Legitimizing royal authority by claiming the ruler is a god or chosen of the gods, binding religious and political power in a single person.
TECH_DRY_DOCK_DESC: A basin that can be flooded to float a ship in and then drained for hull inspection, repair, and caulking.
TECH_DYE_MAKING_DESC: Extracting and fixing plant and mineral pigments into cloth, making color a marker of wealth, identity, and trade.
TECH_FAIENCE_DESC: First noticed when quartz sand fused with natron ash in a kiln accident — the resulting blue-green glassy material became the prestige material of the ancient world.
TECH_FALCONRY_DESC: Training raptors to hunt for their handler, a pursuit that blurred the line between sport and practical hunting assistance.
TECH_FORMAL_EDUCATION_DESC: Establishing scribal schools and academies where knowledge is systematically transmitted to selected students, creating professional intellectual classes.
TECH_FRESCO_PAINTING_DESC: Applying pigment to wet plaster so that colors bond permanently into walls and ceilings as they dry.
TECH_GEOMETRY_DESC: Formalizing the mathematical relationships of space and shape, enabling precise land survey, architecture, and astronomical prediction.
TECH_GLASS_MAKING_DESC: Fusing silica sand with soda and lime at high temperature to produce a transparent, moldable material of extraordinary value.
TECH_GRANARY_ADMIN_DESC: Managing centralized grain stores with records of deposit and withdrawal — the bureaucratic nucleus around which early states coalesced.
TECH_HIEROGLYPHICS_DESC: A writing system combining logographic and alphabetic elements carved or painted on stone and papyrus, preserving Egypt's history for millennia.
TECH_KEEL_CONSTRUCTION_DESC: Adding a structural backbone along a ship's bottom to improve directional stability and allow larger, deeper-hulled vessels.
TECH_LIGHTHOUSE_DESC: A tower with a fire or mirror at its apex marking a harbor entrance, guiding ships safely through darkness and fog.
TECH_LIME_PLASTER_DESC: Burning limestone to produce quicklime, mixed with water into a plaster that hardens to a durable, waterproof surface.
TECH_LOOM_WEAVING_DESC: A frame that holds warp threads under tension while a shuttle passes weft threads through, enabling rapid production of uniform cloth.
TECH_LOST_WAX_CASTING_DESC: Sculpting a wax model, encasing it in clay, and pouring in molten metal — the wax burns away, leaving a precise metal reproduction.
TECH_MAP_MAKING_DESC: Rendering the spatial relationship of places on a surface, externalizing geographic knowledge and enabling planning beyond memory's reach.
TECH_MARKET_SQUARE_DESC: Designating a regular public space and time for trade, transforming occasional barter into a predictable, expanding commercial institution.
TECH_MASK_MAKING_DESC: Crafting ritual masks that allow the wearer to embody a spirit, ancestor, or deity during ceremony.
TECH_MEGALITH_CONSTRUCTION_DESC: Moving and erecting massive stones into monuments, tombs, and astronomical alignments through collective labor of extraordinary ambition.
TECH_MERCHANT_GUILD_DESC: Organizing traders into an association that sets standards, shares risk, and collectively negotiates with rulers and foreign partners.
TECH_MONOCULTURE_TRAP_DESC: Dedicating all fields to a single high-yield crop. Efficient... for now. The land and the people grow equally dependent.
TECH_MOUNTED_ARCHERY_DESC: Shooting a composite bow from horseback at full gallop — a skill demanding years of mastery and producing warriors of terrifying mobility.
TECH_OAR_BANK_DESC: Stacking multiple rows of oarsmen to drive a warship at speed, sacrificing cargo space for raw propulsive power.
TECH_OLIVE_PRESSING_DESC: Crushing olives under weighted stone to extract oil for cooking, lighting, and medicine — the liquid gold of the Mediterranean.
TECH_PALISADE_DESC: Encircling a settlement with sharpened wooden stakes driven into the earth, a fast and effective perimeter defense.
TECH_PAVED_ROAD_DESC: Laying cut stone over an earthen roadbed to create all-weather routes that armies, merchants, and messengers can traverse reliably.
TECH_PHALANX_TACTICS_DESC: Forming infantry into tight interlocking ranks of shields and spears, creating a bristling wall that absorbs and crushes opposing forces.
TECH_PLOW_FARMING_DESC: Using an animal-drawn implement to break and turn soil, dramatically expanding the area a single family can cultivate.
TECH_POLYTHEISM_DESC: A formal pantheon of gods with distinct domains and personalities, serving as a shared cosmological framework for law, morality, and statecraft.
TECH_PROMISSORY_NOTE_DESC: A written pledge to pay a debt at a future date, allowing trade beyond the limits of what one can carry and creating the earliest credit instruments.
TECH_QANAT_DESC: A gently sloping tunnel bored from a hillside aquifer to surface level, delivering a gravity-fed water supply across arid land without pumps.
TECH_RAISED_BED_FARMING_DESC: Mounding soil into elevated growing platforms to improve drainage and concentrate fertility in waterlogged or poor-soil environments.
TECH_RESERVOIR_DESC: Damming a valley or excavating a basin to store rainwater and runoff, providing a year-round water supply independent of seasonal rivers.
TECH_SALINIZATION_TRAP_DESC: Maximizing harvests by flooding fields year-round. The soil grows more fertile each season — until the salt left behind cannot be washed away.
TECH_SEAL_STAMP_DESC: Engraved stones or cylinders pressed into clay to mark ownership and authenticate documents — the earliest signature.
TECH_SEXAGESIMAL_MATH_DESC: A base-sixty number system that simplifies calculation of fractions and angles, still embedded in hours, minutes, and degrees today.
TECH_SHIELD_WALL_DESC: Overlapping shields edge-to-edge across a battle line to form a mobile barrier that infantry advance behind.
TECH_SIEGE_LADDER_DESC: Portable scaling ladders that allow attackers to breach city walls directly, forcing defenders to hold the entire perimeter.
TECH_SILKWORM_RAISING_DESC: Cultivating silkworms on mulberry leaves and carefully reeling fiber from their cocoons to produce the most prized textile in the ancient world.
TECH_SOLAR_CALENDAR_DESC: Tracking the sun's annual cycle with enough precision to predict solstices and equinoxes, synchronizing agriculture, religion, and governance.
TECH_SQUARE_SAIL_DESC: A rectangular canvas hung from a yard that catches following winds, freeing oarsmen and opening the possibility of long ocean passages.
TECH_STANDARD_WEIGHTS_DESC: Establishing official stone and metal weights enforced by the state, making trade fair and fraud detectable.
TECH_STAR_MAP_DESC: Charting the positions and movements of stars into named constellations, providing a navigational and calendrical reference for sailors and farmers alike.
TECH_STEAM_BENDING_WOOD_DESC: Exposing timber to steam to make it pliable enough to be bent into curved shapes for ship ribs, wheel rims, and furniture.
TECH_STONE_MASONRY_DESC: Cutting and dressing stone blocks to precise dimensions for fitting into walls, columns, and monuments of lasting permanence.
TECH_SUNDIAL_DESC: Casting a shadow on a marked surface to read the time of day from the sun's position — the first instrument for measuring the hours.
TECH_SURGERY_DESC: Performing deliberate cuts on the body to remove damaged tissue, set compound fractures, or drain infection — care beyond herbalism.
TECH_SUSPENSION_BRIDGE_DESC: Stringing rope or fiber cables across a gorge and hanging a walkway from them, spanning chasms no stone bridge could cross.
TECH_TAXATION_DESC: Levying a regular portion of production from subjects to fund state functions — armies, roads, temples, and the bureaucracy itself.
TECH_TIDE_PREDICTION_DESC: Observing tidal patterns over years to anticipate high and low water, enabling safe harbor entry and coastal operations.
TECH_TIN_DEPENDENCY_TRAP_DESC: Building an economy and military around bronze alloy is a path to undeniable strength — provided the tin keeps arriving from distant mines.
TECH_TOLL_SYSTEM_DESC: Charging passage fees at bridges, gates, and mountain passes to generate revenue and control the flow of goods and people.
TECH_TYRIAN_PURPLE_DESC: Discovered when murex snails left to rot produced a reddish stain that proved colorfast and impossibly vivid — the rarest dye of antiquity, worth more than gold by weight.
TECH_VETERINARY_DESC: Applying medical knowledge to livestock and working animals, protecting the agricultural and military assets that civilization depends upon.
TECH_WAREHOUSE_DESC: Purpose-built facilities for bulk storage and distribution of goods, enabling merchants and states to buffer supply against demand across distances.
TECH_WINE_MAKING_DESC: Fermenting crushed grapes into wine — safer than water in many climates, valuable in trade, and central to ritual across the ancient world.
TECH_ZIGGURAT_DESC: A tiered mud-brick temple mountain rising above the flat Mesopotamian plain, the physical and spiritual axis of a city-state.
